# Phase 1 Capture — Spec ↔ py-reference Audit (A2)

**Status**: A2 audit, 2026-05-08. Compares blind spec `phase-1-capture.md` v0.1 against `py-reference/cli/trail.py` + `cli/render.py` (and adjacent code). Resolution belongs to A3.
**Method**: read the spec; read py-reference exhaustively; emit a punch-list. Where intent is ambiguous (e.g., is this Python idiom or load-bearing semantics?), it is marked AMBIGUOUS.
**Caveat**: py-reference here is `py-reference/cli/trail.py` *as it exists on disk today*. The canonical fixture packet at `.trail/sessions/18e374b5-.../packet.yml` was generated by an earlier or identical revision; spot checks against that file are noted where relevant.

Legend:
- **L** = Load-bearing (functional contract; affects packet shape, validity, or semantics)
- **Q** = Quality-of-life (CLI ergonomics, output prettiness, debug)
- **A** = AMBIGUOUS — could be either

---

## Category A — Spec gaps (py-reference does X; spec missed it OR covered it incorrectly)

| # | py-reference location | What it does | L/Q/A | Recommendation |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| A1 | `trail.py:18` `VERSION = "0.1.0-dev"` | Hard-coded version constant; emitted into `_meta.generator.version`. | L | **Refine spec.** Spec §4 says "from `package.json` version" — that's the right answer for TS, but the audit notes the actual value the parity fixture contains is `"0.1.0-dev"`. Decide: bump TS package to `0.1.0-dev` for parity, OR document this as an allowed parity diff. |
| A2 | `trail.py:19` `SCHEMA_URL = "schema/pr-change-packet.v0.1.yml"` | Constant relative path, not a URL. | L | **Spec wrong (or refine).** Spec §4 says `_meta.schema_url` is a "constant pointer to … URL". Fixture has the relative path. Choose: keep relative path (matches fixture) and reword spec, OR change to a real URL and accept parity diff. |
| A3 | `trail.py:447` `packet_id` uses `uuid.uuid4()` | Generates UUIDv4, not ULID. | L | **Spec vs py-reference disagree.** Spec §4 says ULID; schema says "ULID or UUIDv7". Recommend: TS port uses ULID per spec; treat as expected parity diff (already covered by §10 since `_meta.packet_id` is allowed-to-differ). |
| A4 | `trail.py:444-452` `_meta` includes `capture_method: "post_hoc"` and (after main) `claim_synthesis: <mode>` | Two extra `_meta` keys not declared in schema or spec. | L | **Add to spec.** Schema doesn't define them; py-reference and the fixture both emit them. Either (a) extend schema additively in v0.1.1 and lock them in, or (b) drop them in TS. Recommended: keep both — `capture_method` clarifies post-hoc vs hook-time; `claim_synthesis` is the run-level synthesis flag the spec §11 was already pondering. |
| A5 | `trail.py:120-141` builds `tool_results` map keyed by `tool_use_id` | Pre-pass scan of all `user`-typed records pulling `tool_result` blocks out of `message.content` — these are how Claude Code transmits tool stdout/stderr back. | L | **Add to spec §4.** Spec says tool results live in `~/.claude/projects/.../<id>/tool-results/*` files; py-reference reads them inline from the transcript via `tool_result` blocks on `user` messages. The directory-based path mentioned in spec §4 is *not* used by py-reference. Recommendation: TS port reads inline `tool_result` blocks (matching fixture); demote the file-based path to "fallback if present" or drop. |
| A6 | `trail.py:164` `re.sub(r"<[^>]+>", "", text).strip()` on user messages | Strips HTML/XML-style tags from prompts before storing. | L | **Add to spec §5/§4.** This is a non-obvious normalization and changes prompt text. Spec is silent. TS port must replicate; otherwise prompts containing Claude's bracketed system tags (`<system-reminder>`, `<command-name>` etc.) won't be redacted/stored identically. Parity oracle will fail without it. |
| A7 | `trail.py:163-167` empty-prompt skip after tag-strip | If `stripped` is empty post-strip, skip the prompt entirely (no `prompt_n++`). | L | **Add to spec.** Affects prompt count and PROMPT-NNN numbering. |
| A8 | `trail.py:170` `redactor.redact(stripped[:MAX_PROMPT_CHARS])` with `MAX_PROMPT_CHARS = 1200` | Truncates prompts to 1200 chars BEFORE redaction. | L | **Add to spec.** Spec §4 doesn't specify a per-prompt cap. Truncation order matters: truncate-then-redact can create dangling `[REDACTED:` markers if a match crosses the boundary; py-reference accepts that. Recommend: spec records 1200-char cap and keeps "truncate then redact" order for parity (or fixes both). |
| A9 | `trail.py:32-35` `MAX_CMD_CHARS=500`, `MAX_STDOUT_CHARS=1200`, `MAX_EXCERPT_CHARS=1200` | Per-field truncation caps for command, stdout summary, and excerpt. | L | **Add to spec.** Same parity logic as A8. |
| A10 | `trail.py:98-103` `_clip()` excerpt elision marker text | Excerpt elision uses `\n... [elided N chars] ...\n` between `head=MAX-200` and `tail=150`. Note the spec quotes `[... elided N chars ...]` but py-reference emits `... [elided N chars] ...` (no leading dots before "elided", different bracket placement). | L | **Spec wrong on string format.** Pick one and lock. Recommend: match py-reference exactly (`... [elided N chars] ...`) for parity. The schema YAML comment on line 98 of `pr-change-packet.v0.1.yml` says `[... elided N chars ...]`. So **schema, spec, and py-reference all disagree.** A3 picks one. |
| A11 | `trail.py:101-103` excerpt elision asymmetry | Head = `MAX-200` chars, tail = `150` chars. Spec says "longer content gets `[... elided N chars ...]` middle marker" — no head/tail split. | L | **Add to spec.** Without exact head/tail split, parity output differs. |
| A12 | `trail.py:25-30` `TEST_COMMAND_RE` | Test detection regex includes: `pytest`, `jest`, `mocha`, `vitest`, `cargo test`, `npm (test|run test)`, `yarn (test|run test)`, `go test`, `rspec`, `phpunit`, **`validate_canvas.py`**, **`validate-template.sh`**. | L (mostly), Q (last two) | **Refine spec.** Spec §4 says "heuristic detection from Bash commands matching test runners (`npm test`, `pytest`, `cargo test`, `go test`, etc.)". The "etc." hides three things: (a) `mocha`/`vitest`/`yarn (test|run test)`/`rspec`/`phpunit` are also matchers; (b) `validate_canvas.py` and `validate-template.sh` are project-specific Mycelium probes — **incidental** for the protocol but they appear in the canonical fixture's TEST entries. (c) The regex is case-insensitive. TS port must replicate exact list to pass parity, or A3 picks a smaller portable list and accepts a parity-fixture re-roll. |
| A13 | `trail.py:196-200` Bash command always emits `exit_code: 0` and `duration_ms: 0` | py-reference does NOT extract real exit codes or durations from tool_result. Sets both to literal 0. | L | **Spec wrong / overstated.** Spec §4 says `test_evidence.passed/failed/not_run` is determined by "Bash commands matching test runners AND their exit codes." py-reference cannot determine exit codes (always 0), so EVERY test always lands in `passed`. `failed` and `not_run` are always empty arrays in py-reference. A3: relax spec to match, OR upgrade TS to actually parse exit codes from tool_result text (Claude Code embeds non-zero exit info in the result body). Recommend the latter (real value), but call out as a behavior diff vs fixture. |
| A14 | `trail.py:199` `stderr_summary: ""` always | py-reference never populates stderr_summary; tool_result is concatenated into stdout_summary only. | L | **Spec wrong.** Spec §5 says `commands_run[*].stderr_summary` is a redacted field; in py-reference it is always empty. A3: keep current behavior (Claude Code mixes stderr into the result text), OR attempt to split. Recommend keep empty (parity) and document. |
| A15 | `trail.py:201-207` test entry shape | `test_evidence.passed[N]` is `{id, ref, cmd_ref}` where `ref = cmd[:140]` and `cmd_ref` points back to the CMD ID. Schema/spec only define `id` and `ref`. | L | **Add to spec / extend schema.** `cmd_ref` is a useful back-pointer; fixture has it. Either schema-bump or drop. |
| A16 | `trail.py:209-263` Edit/Write/MultiEdit excerpt construction | • `Write` produces single excerpt `kind="after"` and description `Wrote {fp} ({len(ctext)} chars)`. • `Edit` produces `before` then `after` excerpts and description `Edited {fp}`. • `MultiEdit` caps to first **5 hunks** and produces `before#N`/`after#N` excerpts with description `MultiEdit on {fp} ({len(edits)} hunk(s))`. | L | **Add to spec §4.** Spec §4 says "one per Edit/Write/MultiEdit" and references "before/after" but does not specify (a) 5-hunk cap on MultiEdit, (b) the `before#N`/`after#N` kind naming, (c) the exact description format `Wrote/Edited/MultiEdit on {fp} (...)`, (d) Write-only-after vs Edit-both-sides. All are required for parity. |
| A17 | `trail.py:139` `MultiEdit` cap silently drops hunks beyond #5 | No record kept of dropped hunks. | A | **Refine spec.** Decide: (a) keep silent cap (matches fixture), (b) record dropped count as metadata, (c) raise the cap. Probably (a) for parity, (b) is the more honest answer. |
| A18 | `trail.py:215-218` `modules_touched` derived from path under `REPO_ROOT` | Splits the file path on `/` after stripping repo root and uses the FIRST segment. Files outside repo root contribute NOTHING to `modules_touched`. | L | **Refine spec.** Spec §4 says "dedup'd top-level dirs of changed files". Fixture's `modules_touched` lists `.claude, .trail, bin, cli, schema` — files under `~/.claude/projects/...` (outside repo) are excluded. Lock this behavior in. |
| A19 | `trail.py:267` `files: [fp]` always single-element | Each semantic_change carries exactly one file. Spec §4 example excerpt and schema both allow `files: []` to be multi. py-reference never produces multi-file. | L | **Refine spec.** Single-file per semantic_change is the de-facto rule. Lock it or change. |
| A20 | `trail.py:268` `tool: tool_name` carried in intermediate dict; emitted as `operation: tool.lower()` at line 498 | Operation is stored as `tool_name.lower()` → e.g., `"write"`, `"edit"`, `"multiedit"`. | L | **Add to spec.** Spec §4 does not say lowercase, and schema example shows `operation: "edit"`. Lock the lowercase convention. |
| A21 | `trail.py:441-442` `summary` field of task_intent | `summary_seed = re.sub(r"\s+", " ", initial_prompt_text)[:200]` — collapses whitespace, truncates to 200 chars. Built from FULL initial prompt text (post-redaction, post-1200-truncation). | L | **Refine spec.** Spec §4 says "first user message, truncated to a single line, redacted". 200-char cap is undocumented; whitespace collapse is undocumented. |
| A22 | `trail.py:442-447` `_meta.packet_id` placement at line 447 | Generated AFTER all parsing/extraction, NOT at session start. (Schema comment at line 28 says "generated by Trail at session start"; py-reference generates at finalization.) | L | **Schema is wrong; spec matches py-reference.** Spec §4 says "ULID generated at packet-build time" — that matches py-reference. Schema's "session start" is aspirational from a hook-driven design that was never built. Keep py-reference behavior; consider correcting schema comment in v0.1.1. |
| A23 | `trail.py:453-460` `pr` block populated entirely empty | `repository, branch, base_branch, author` all empty strings; `pr_number` null. py-reference NEVER calls simple-git or `git config user.email`. | L | **Spec significantly diverges from py-reference.** Spec §4 mandates simple-git for `pr.repository`, `pr.branch`, `pr.base_branch`, `pr.author`, and `diff_summary.base_sha`/`head_sha`/`files_changed`/`lines_added`/`lines_deleted`. py-reference does NOT. The fixture has empty pr.* and `files_changed: 26` (length of files_changed set, not git diff stat) and `lines_added: 0, lines_deleted: 0`. A3 must decide: (a) spec wins → TS adds simple-git → parity fixture must be re-rolled, (b) py-reference wins → drop simple-git from spec. Recommend (a): adding git state is a real product improvement; mark this as an explicit, documented parity diff. **This is the single biggest gap.** |
| A24 | `trail.py:489` `files_changed: len(data["files_changed"])` | Uses count of distinct files touched by Edit/Write/MultiEdit tool uses, NOT git diff stat. | L | Same as A23. Spec says diff stats; py-reference does counted set. |
| A25 | `trail.py:467-485` `agent_session.transcript_summary: []` always empty | Never populated by py-reference. | L | **Spec ambiguous; py-reference de-facto empty.** Spec §4 says "bullet list. Either LLM-synthesized … or mechanical (from tool-use names + first lines of assistant messages)." py-reference produces neither. A3: relax (allow empty) or implement. Recommend: implement in TS — it's a useful field and the fixture's empty array is not load-bearing. Mark as documented parity diff. |
| A26 | `trail.py:511-518` `provenance` fields | `ai_generated_estimate: "high"` (constant), `human_modified_estimate: "unknown"` (constant), `method: "post-hoc-transcript"` (constant). `agent_touched_files = data["files_changed"]`. `human_touched_files: []` always. | L | **Spec wrong / overstated.** Spec §4 says "hook-tracked if Claude Code session hooks fired; heuristic otherwise" and `human_touched_files` modified during session window but not touched by tool uses. py-reference has neither hook tracking nor heuristic — they're constants. A3: keep constants (parity) or implement heuristic. Recommend: keep as constants for v0.1.0; document. |
| A27 | `trail.py:285-302` mechanical synthesis groups by FILE, not by DIFF | Spec §6 says mechanical mode emits "one claim per `semantic_changes[*]` entry". py-reference groups multiple DIFFs of the same file into ONE claim with merged evidence_refs and a description like `edit/write on {fp} ({n} change(s))`. | L | **Spec wrong.** Fixture confirms file-grouped claims (e.g., CLAIM-008 has 11 evidence refs all for the same file). A3 must pick. Recommend: spec adopts py-reference's per-file grouping (it's what the fixture has and what produces a sane claim count for big sessions). |
| A28 | `trail.py:304-312` mechanical synthesis appends an extra "Ran N test/validation command(s)" claim | When `data["tests"]` is non-empty, mechanical mode appends one final claim referencing all TEST-NNN ids. | L | **Add to spec.** Spec §6 says claims = one per semantic_change. The test-roll-up claim is a real py-reference behavior and appears in the fixture (CLAIM-027). |
| A29 | `trail.py:300` mechanical claim text format | `f"{'/'.join(actions)} on {fp} ({len(ds)} change(s))"` where `actions = sorted({tool.lower() for d in ds})`. | L | **Add to spec.** This exact string format is what the parity fixture contains; TS port must reproduce it byte-for-byte. |
| A30 | `trail.py:295-302` mechanical claim always `confidence: "supported"` | py-reference does NOT use the `>=2 evidence supported / =1 partial / 0 ungrounded` rule the spec §6 describes. ALL mechanical claims are "supported". | L | **Spec wrong.** Either (a) keep py-reference's blanket "supported" (matches fixture), or (b) implement the gradient. Recommend (a) for parity; gradient can land in v0.2. |
| A31 | `trail.py:301` mechanical claim `synthesis: "mechanical"` field | Each claim has a `synthesis` (NOT `synthesis_mode`) field with value `"mechanical"` or `"llm"`. | L | **Spec/Schema gap.** Spec §11 raised this tension; py-reference resolved it by adding a non-schema field `synthesis` per claim. A3 picks: (a) extend schema additively with `synthesis_mode`, (b) keep `synthesis` as-is, (c) move to `_meta.claim_synthesis` (which py-reference ALSO populates redundantly). Recommend (a) + drop the `_meta.claim_synthesis` redundancy. |
| A32 | `trail.py:317-428` LLM mode: `claude` subprocess invocation differs significantly from spec | Spec §6 says: stdin is "structured prompt containing transcript_summary + mechanical claims + evidence refs + instruction to produce 5–15 claims, each citing ≥1 evidence ref, never inventing IDs", with "delimited claim text + evidence_refs lines". py-reference: prompt is an English instruction asking for ONE sentence per file group, output as a JSON array `[{"file": "...", "claim": "..."}]`. No mechanical-claim seeding. No 5-15 range. Evidence_refs are NOT model-chosen — they're inferred mechanically from `file → diff_ids[]` mapping after parse. | L | **Spec wrong / outdated.** py-reference's design (LLM picks the *text*, code picks the *evidence*) is sounder — it eliminates "model invents evidence IDs" risk entirely. Recommend: A3 rewrites spec §6 LLM section to match py-reference, and the spec's invariant "model never invents IDs" is preserved by construction. |
| A33 | `trail.py:368-373` `claude` CLI args | Hard-coded `--max-budget-usd 0.50` (NOT plumbed from a CLI flag), `--no-session-persistence`, `--output-format text`, `timeout=120` seconds, no `--no-llm-strict` equivalent. | L | **Refine spec §3.** Spec promises `--llm-budget-usd <number>` (default 0.50) and `--llm-model <model>`. py-reference plumbs `--llm-model` (yes) but NOT `--llm-budget-usd` (no — it's hardcoded). A3: spec wins (TS plumbs both flags). Also: 120-second timeout is undocumented; lock or refine. |
| A34 | `trail.py:382-393` LLM output post-processing | Strips ` ```json ` and ` ``` ` fences from output. Re-tries JSON parse on the de-fenced string. Bails to mechanical with descriptive error. | Q | Spec is silent; py-reference's behavior is reasonable. **Add to spec** as a SHOULD ("LLM output may include fences; strip before parsing"). |
| A35 | `trail.py:425-426` LLM mode falls back to mechanical when claims-count < file-groups-count | If LLM returned fewer claims than groups, py-reference treats it as failure and falls back. | L | **Add to spec §6.** This is a hidden quality gate. TS port must replicate, OR replace with a different gate. |
| A36 | `trail.py:415-423` LLM mode ALSO appends the "Ran N test/validation" mechanical claim | Even in LLM mode, the test-rollup claim is mechanical. The `synthesis` field is `"mechanical"` for that one claim. | L | **Add to spec §6.** Mixed-synthesis output is real. |
| A37 | `trail.py:526-535` Layer 2 validation | Serializes packet to YAML, replaces `[REDACTED:...]` markers with empty string, then re-runs every regex against the scrubbed text. Captures `pattern + first-40-chars-of-match` (NOT 12 as spec §5 says). | L | **Spec wrong on length.** py-reference uses `m.group(0)[:40]`. Spec §5 says "first-12-chars-of-match (NOT the full match — sensitive!)". A3: pick a number. 12 is safer; 40 gives more debug context. Recommend 12 (security tilt). |
| A38 | `trail.py:528` Layer 2 serializes with `default_flow_style=False, sort_keys=False` | Specific YAML dump options; NOT mentioned in spec. Affects the serialized bytes that Layer 2 then scans. | L | **Add to spec.** Both Layer 2 (validation) and the actual write at `trail.py:568` use these YAML options. The TS port using `js-yaml` must produce byte-identical output for parity, or the fixture diff explodes. (`width=120, allow_unicode=True` also at line 568.) |
| A39 | `trail.py:565-566` validation_errors written AFTER initial packet build | py-reference builds packet → validates → writes back validation_errors to packet → writes file. Spec §5 wording suggests Layer 2 runs over "about-to-be-written packet bytes" — order is OK, but spec doesn't acknowledge that running Layer 2 mutates the packet (back-fills validation_errors), and Layer 2 then runs again over the now-mutated packet on disk? py-reference does NOT re-validate after writing back errors. | A | **Add to spec.** Define the write order. py-reference's order: serialize → scan → write findings into in-memory packet → re-serialize → write to disk. Layer 2 does NOT re-scan post-write; the on-disk bytes may technically differ from the bytes scanned. Recommend acknowledge + accept (the structural diff is the validation_errors array; nothing secret-bearing changes). |
| A40 | `trail.py:529` `redaction_marker_re = re.compile(r"\[REDACTED:[a-z0-9-]+\]")` | Layer 2 strips redaction markers BEFORE scanning, so a marker like `[REDACTED:aws-access-key]` doesn't itself match a pattern. | L | **Add to spec §5.** Without this, the validator would over-report. |
| A41 | `trail.py:530` Layer 2 uses `regex.search` (not `findall`) | First match wins; subsequent matches under the same pattern are not enumerated. | L | **Refine spec §5.** Spec says "if any pattern matches, append … to validation_errors". py-reference appends one entry per pattern, NOT per match. Lock or change. |
| A42 | `trail.py:538-555` CLI uses argparse | Subcommand structure: `trail packet generate <session_id> [--out PATH] [--no-llm] [--llm-model MODEL]`. NO `--latest` flag, NO `--llm-budget-usd`, NO `--output`/dir flag, NO `--format`, NO `--dry-run`, NO `--no-storage`, NO `--quiet`, NO `--strict-llm`, NO `trail packet list`, NO `trail --version`. `--out` defaults to stdout (`-`). | L | **Spec dramatically extends CLI.** Spec §3 lists 9 commands+flags that py-reference doesn't have. A3: most are sensible additions (TS port adds them); flag the missing ones explicitly. Critically, the spec's "default behavior (no flags) → finds latest session, writes to `.trail/sessions/.../packet.yml`" does NOT exist in py-reference (always requires session_id, defaults to stdout). |
| A43 | `trail.py:570-576` output behavior | `--out -` → stdout; `--out path` → mkdir parents, writepath. py-reference does NOT write `packet.md`. NO storage stub. NO `.trail/sessions/<id>/` default. | L | **Spec extends.** Render is a separate script (`render.py`) the user runs manually. A3: TS port unifies (single command emits both yml + md). |
| A44 | `trail.py:578-584` Stderr summary line format | `f"records={N} prompts={N} commands={N} diffs={N} tests={N} redactions={N} validation_errors={N} synthesis={mode}"` | Q | **Refine spec §3/§8.** Spec §8 exit-0 says one-line summary `packet <packet-id> for session <session-id> ({N} claims, {M} redactions) → .trail/sessions/...`. Different shape entirely. A3: pick. Recommend the spec's shape — more useful info. |
| A45 | `trail.py:75-81` `find_transcript()` | Walks every project_dir under `~/.claude/projects/` looking for `<session_id>.jsonl`. Does NOT compute the sanitized cwd. Does NOT short-circuit on cwd match. | L | **Refine spec §4.** Spec says "finds the latest session for the cwd at `~/.claude/projects/<sanitized-cwd>/`". py-reference is cwd-insensitive — given a session_id it finds it anywhere. A3: TS port could be either; for `--latest` the spec's cwd-scoped behavior is required. Add cwd scoping for `--latest`; keep "search all" for explicit session_id. |
| A46 | `trail.py:73-74` `find_transcript()` raises `FileNotFoundError(f"{CLAUDE_PROJECTS_ROOT} does not exist")` | Distinct error case for missing root vs missing session. | Q | Maps to spec exit code 2 "transcript not found". Locate as separate sub-message; py-reference's two messages are reasonable. |
| A47 | `trail.py:84-95` `parse_transcript()` silently skips JSONDecodeError lines | Best-effort parse; malformed lines dropped with no log. | A | **Add to spec.** Spec is silent on malformed transcript lines. Recommend: keep silent skip (matches py-reference); document. |
| A48 | `trail.py:106-117` `_flatten_text_blocks()` | Joins text blocks of a content array with `\n`. Only `type: "text"` blocks counted; `tool_use`/`tool_result`/`thinking`/`image`/etc. are dropped. | L | **Add to spec.** TS port must do exactly this for prompt parity. |
| A49 | `trail.py:146-151` first-encounter wins for started_at, last-encounter wins for ended_at, but uses string comparison on timestamps | `if started_at is None or ts < started_at` — string-lex compare. Works for ISO-8601 but is fragile for non-Z timezone offsets. Fixture has mixed `'2026-05-08T11:36:32.933Z'` and `'2026-05-08T15:46:59.455Z'`. | A | **Refine spec.** TS port should parse to Date and compare properly; for parity output, format ISO-8601 string identical to py-reference. |
| A50 | `trail.py:174-176` `model = mdl` always overwrites | Last assistant model wins, not first. Spec §4 says "first assistant message's model field". Fixture has `claude-opus-4-7` (the model from the LAST message — many sessions have model switches, /effort changes etc.). | L | **Spec wrong.** A3 picks: (a) match py-reference (last wins), (b) match spec text (first wins), (c) record list of all models seen. Recommend (a) for parity; (c) is the more honest answer. |
| A51 | `trail.py:177-180` assistant content type-guard | `if not isinstance(content, list): continue` — assistant messages with string content are silently skipped. | A | **Add to spec.** Real Claude transcripts almost always have list content for assistants, but this guard exists. |
| A52 | `trail.py:213-218` files_changed accumulator | Includes file paths for ALL Edit/Write/MultiEdit tool uses, even paths outside the repo (e.g., `~/.claude/projects/...`). modules_touched only includes inside-repo. | L | **Add to spec.** files_changed has a wider net than modules_touched. Both are visible in the fixture. |
| A53 | `trail.py:220-263` `Write` tool description includes byte length: `Wrote {fp} ({len(ctext)} chars)` | Length is char count of pre-redaction string. | L | Add to spec (per A16 already covers shape). |
| A54 | `trail.py:55` `Redactor.counts: dict` initialized as `{}` (insertion-ordered, then sorted at emit) | py-reference emits `redactions_by_pattern` via `dict(sorted(redactor.counts.items()))` — alphabetical pattern_name keys. | L | **Add to spec.** Output stability for parity. |
| A55 | `trail.py:481` `redactions_by_pattern: dict(sorted(...))` | (Same as A54.) Alphabetical sort order. | L | (Folded with A54.) |
| A56 | `trail.py:38-47` `load_patterns` reads from `REPO_ROOT/bin/trail-redaction-patterns.yml` | `REPO_ROOT = Path(__file__).resolve().parent.parent.parent` — three parents up from `cli/trail.py`. Hard-coded; not configurable. | L | **Add to spec.** TS port resolution: package-bundled file vs repo-relative. Spec §4 says "Distributed with `@trail/capture` package; user-overrideable via `--patterns <path>` (deferred)." Lock the package-bundled path; document fallback behavior. |
| A57 | `trail.py:42-46` pattern compile loop | Uses Python `re` (not `regex` module). No flags applied EXCEPT what's inline in patterns (e.g., `(?i)`). | L | **Add to spec.** TS port uses native `RegExp`. Differences in regex engines may produce parity drift on certain patterns; explicitly test each pattern. |
| A58 | `trail.py:63-66` redactor uses closure over `name` via default-arg trick `_n=name` | Python loop-closure idiom; not relevant to behavior. | Q | Incidental; see Category B. |
| A59 | `trail.py:120-141` tool_results pre-pass | Iterates ALL records before main loop. Two-pass design. Memory cost is OK for ≤10MB transcripts; could be O(N) streaming for ≥50MB. | A | **Add to spec.** Spec §9 mentions streaming for ≥50MB pathological case. py-reference is two-pass in-memory. Lock or refine. |
| A60 | `trail.py:556` `find_transcript` then `parse_transcript` then `load_patterns` order | Specific ordering: transcript first, patterns second. If transcript missing → exit early with FileNotFoundError; patterns never loaded. | A | Ordering doesn't affect output but does affect error precedence. Spec §8 lists exit codes 2 (transcript) before 4 (patterns) — matches py-reference. |
| A61 | `trail.py:556-585` no try/except wrapping → uncaught exceptions print Python traceback to stderr and exit nonzero | py-reference does NOT catch any errors; ALL non-zero exits are uncaught Python exceptions with full traceback. | L | **Spec wrong about exit codes.** Spec §8 defines exit codes 2-8 with specific stderr shapes. py-reference ONLY produces these accidentally for FileNotFoundError (2) — patterns missing → exit 1 with `FileNotFoundError` traceback (NOT exit 4 with the friendly message). Schema validation failure (5) and writability (6) are similarly uncaught. A3: spec wins, TS port adds proper error handling. Treat as known-divergent behavior. |
| A62 | (no `cli/__init__.py`) | py-reference `cli/` directory has no `__init__.py`. `trail.py` and `render.py` are standalone scripts. | Q | Incidental; not a Python package. TS organization is independent. |
| A63 | `render.py:13-29` `_lang_for()` mapping | Maps file extensions to syntax-highlight language hint for fenced code blocks. Specific list: py, yml/yaml, md, json, js/jsx, ts/tsx, sh, rs, go. | Q | **Add to spec / Appendix C.** Markdown render policy. Spec defers MD information architecture to Phase 2. Lock list or document the omission. |
| A64 | `render.py:31-44` `_render_excerpts()` | Marker char `−` for `before*` kinds, `+` for everything else. Surrounds content with fenced code block. Adds `*(elided)*` italic suffix when `elided: true`. | L (for MD output) | **Add to spec.** If TS port emits packet.md, this format must match for `format=both` parity (if md-parity is in scope; spec is silent on md byte-parity). |
| A65 | `render.py:75-78` validation_errors warning | Emits a `> ⚠ Redaction Layer 2 reported issues …` blockquote when validation_errors non-empty, with bullet per error. | L | **Add to spec.** |
| A66 | `render.py:90-92` `diff_by_id`/`cmd_by_id`/`test_by_id` lookup tables | Built once before claim render loop. | Q | Implementation detail; OK either way. |
| A67 | `render.py:105-144` claim render loop produces inline anchor sections per evidence ref | Each evidence ref renders inline below the claim heading: DIFF → operation+file+excerpts; CMD → command+stdout fence; TEST → ref. Unresolved refs render `_(unresolved)_`. | L (for MD) | **Add to spec/Appendix C.** This is the F1 fix — claims render with inline evidence. |
| A68 | `render.py:147-191` orphan evidence appendix | Lists evidence not cited by any claim, capped (40 cmds, 30 diffs, 30 tests) and folded into `<details>`. | L (for MD) | **Add to spec.** Appendix structure is part of the human-readable contract. |
| A69 | `render.py:193-200` initial prompt block | Adds `<a id='prompt-001'></a>` anchor + 1000-char truncated initial prompt in code fence. | L (for MD) | Add to spec. |
| A70 | `render.py:202-203` footer line | `*Generated by Trail v0.1 (post-hoc) from <packet_path>.*` | Q | Add to spec. |
| A71 | `render.py:207-225` separate CLI binary `trail-render` | Standalone tool: `trail-render <packet_path> [--out -|path] [--format human|agent]`. agent format = passthrough YAML. | L | **Spec change.** Spec unifies into `trail packet generate --format md|yaml|both`; py-reference splits into two CLIs. Recommend spec wins; render becomes a library function callable from both `trail packet generate` and a future `trail packet render` subcommand. |
| A72 | `render.py:217` `agent` format = raw passthrough of packet.yml | No re-serialization; reads file text and writes it. | Q | Curious but harmless. Drop in TS port (the user already has packet.yml). |
| A73 | `trail.py:163` HTML/XML tag strip BEFORE truncation | Order: stripped tags → check empty → truncate to 1200 → redact. | L | Add to spec; see A6/A8. |
| A74 | `trail-audit-precommit:22-41` Layer 3 implementation | Uses SAME `REDACTION_MARKER_RE` strip-then-scan as Layer 2. Walks `.trail/` recursively. Reports `path:line pattern=name match=<60-char>` per finding. **Phase 3a per spec — SKIP for Phase 1 audit.** | — | Out of scope per spec §2 / §5. Noted for completeness. |
| A75 | `trail.py:539-550` argparse subparser ordering | `trail packet generate <session_id>` is the ONLY working command. `trail packet` alone with no subcommand exits 2 (argparse default). `trail` alone exits 2. | L | **Refine spec §3.** Spec §3 says `trail` alone produces top-level help; argparse default is exit 2. TS port should match spec (top-level help → exit 0). |
| A76 | `trail.py:540-544` argparse `required=True` on subparsers | Forces subcommand. | Q | TS port matches via commander/cac. |
| A77 | `trail.py:543` `p_gen.add_argument("session_id")` is positional, no `--latest` | Spec adds `--latest` mode. | L | See A42. |
| A78 | `trail.py:163-167` user message detection ignores any `tool_result`-only user records | A `user`-typed record whose content is a list of pure `tool_result` blocks produces no flattened text → stripped is empty → skipped. | L | **Add to spec.** Otherwise tool_result records would be miscounted as prompts. |

---

## Category B — Incidental implementation (py-reference does X; not load-bearing)

| # | py-reference location | What & why incidental | Recommendation |
|---|---|---|---|
| B1 | `trail.py:18` `VERSION = "0.1.0-dev"` literal | Hand-rolled version constant. TS uses `package.json#version`. | Drop the literal; bind to `package.json`. |
| B2 | `trail.py:21-23` `REPO_ROOT = Path(__file__).resolve().parent.parent.parent` | Three-parents-up trick that breaks the moment the file moves under `py-reference/`. (Confirmation: `/Users/danielbentes/trail/py-reference/cli/trail.py` → `parent.parent.parent` is `/Users/danielbentes/trail/` ✓ — still works by happy accident.) | TS port uses the package's bundled-asset resolution. Ignore the Python idiom. |
| B3 | `trail.py:38-47` patterns loaded relative to `REPO_ROOT` | Same idiom as B2; the loaded YAML doesn't actually have a `version` default in code (the YAML has `version: "0.1.1"` but `data.get("version", "0.0.0")` exists "just in case"). | Drop the `0.0.0` fallback in TS — schema requires non-empty pattern_set_version. Fail loudly. |
| B4 | `trail.py:50-69` `Redactor` as a class with mutable counters | OO holdover; could be a closure or module-level state. | TS port: pure-function redactor returning `{redactedText, counts}` — easier to test. |
| B5 | `trail.py:63-66` default-arg closure trick `_n=name` | Pure Python idiom for late-binding loop variables. | TS doesn't have this issue (let-bound `name`). Skip. |
| B6 | `trail.py:84-95` `parse_transcript` reads entire file into memory | Two-pass design (also B7). Fine for ≤10MB; would need streaming for spec §9 pathological case. | TS port: stream `readline` is trivial in node; do that from the start. |
| B7 | `trail.py:120-141` two-pass over records (tool_results pre-pass + main pass) | Avoids forward-reference of tool_use IDs. Could be one-pass with deferred resolution. | TS port may keep two-pass; not a bottleneck for v0.1. |
| B8 | `trail.py:128-129` `if rec.get("type") != "user": continue` — only `user`-typed records carry tool_results | Claude Code convention. Locked. | Replicate. |
| B9 | `trail.py:135-141` if `tr_content` is a list, join the `text` field of each block | Robust against Claude Code transmitting tool_results as either string or list-of-blocks. | Replicate. |
| B10 | `trail.py:201-207` `cmd[:140]` for `test_evidence.passed[*].ref` | 140-char cap on test refs. Looks like a Twitter-era number. | Document the cap (pick same number for parity, or change to a sensible value like full-or-200 chars). Recommend keep 140. |
| B11 | `trail.py:249` `edits[:5]` MultiEdit hunk cap | (Already noted A17.) | Drop or document; see A17. |
| B12 | `trail.py:317-318` "Falls back to mechanical on error" docstring on `synthesize_claims_llm` | Docstring is the only place fallback semantics are documented inline. | Move to TS doc comments / spec. |
| B13 | `trail.py:330-340` `excerpts_preview` 200-char-per-excerpt cap, 3-diff-cap, 2-excerpt-per-diff cap, 1200-char total cap on the prompt-sample-section | All hand-tuned to keep `claude` CLI prompt under some token budget. Specific numbers are incidental. | TS port: pick its own budget; document. |
| B14 | `trail.py:342` `data["prompts"][0]["text"][:600]` | 600-char cap on initial prompt sample sent to LLM. | Same as B13. |
| B15 | `trail.py:344-364` prompt template English text | Long English instruction. Specific wording is non-load-bearing as long as JSON output works. | TS port uses its own prompt; tune for whichever model. |
| B16 | `trail.py:367-374` `subprocess.run` with `capture_output=True, text=True, timeout=120` | Standard subprocess invocation. | TS uses `child_process.spawn`; same idea. |
| B17 | `trail.py:383-386` regex strip of code fences | Defensive; LLM occasionally returns fenced JSON. | Replicate. |
| B18 | `trail.py:392-393` `if not isinstance(parsed, list)` | Defensive type check. | Replicate. |
| B19 | `trail.py:395` `by_file_path = {fg["file"]: fg for fg in file_groups}` | Lookup hashmap. | Implementation detail. |
| B20 | `trail.py:432-437` `synthesize_claims` wrapper | Tries LLM → on failure, prints stderr msg → falls back to mechanical. The stderr print is debug-y. | Add a `--quiet` gate per spec. |
| B21 | `trail.py:436` `print(f"LLM synthesis failed: {err}; falling back to mechanical", file=sys.stderr)` | Verbose debug; hidden by spec's `--quiet` flag. | Make conditional on `--quiet`. |
| B22 | `trail.py:444` literal `"packet_version": "0.1"` | Hard-coded constant. | Spec locks to `"0.1"`; OK. |
| B23 | `trail.py:448` `datetime.now(timezone.utc).isoformat()` | UTC timestamp w/ tzinfo. | Replicate format exactly (must end with `+00:00`, not `Z`). |
| B24 | `trail.py:454` `provider: "github"` constant | Hardcoded; matches schema default. | Replicate. |
| B25 | `trail.py:521` `sum(1 for c in claims if not c["evidence_refs"])` | Counts ungrounded claims; in py-reference always 0 (mechanical always emits evidence). | Replicate; spec §6 invariant says ≥1 ref always. |
| B26 | `trail.py:528` `width=120, allow_unicode=True` | YAML dump options. (Critical for parity — see A38.) | NOT incidental. Already covered in A38. (Listed here only to redirect.) |
| B27 | `render.py:13-15` `_fence(lang="")` helper | Simple helper. | Inline in TS or keep. |
| B28 | `render.py:71-73` redaction summary line | One-line summary in MD header. | Replicate. |
| B29 | `render.py:165-191` orphan caps `[:40]`, `[:30]`, `[:30]` | Hand-tuned caps to keep MD render skim-able. | Pick same numbers for parity; document. |
| B30 | `render.py:198` `sess["prompts"]["initial"][:1000]` | 1000-char cap on initial prompt in render. | Replicate. |
| B31 | `render.py:217` agent format passthrough | (Already A72.) Incidental — drop. | Drop. |
| B32 | `render.py:213` `--format` choices `["human", "agent"]` | Different from spec's `yaml|md|both`. | Spec's vocabulary wins. |
| B33 | `trail.py:563` `packet["_meta"]["claim_synthesis"] = synth_mode` after build | Mutates packet post-build. Incidental — could be set inside `build_packet`. | TS: set inside builder. |
| B34 | `trail.py:566` validation_errors written to packet AFTER initial build, BEFORE serialization | Spec §5 ambivalent on order. py-reference order is OK. | Lock order in TS. |
| B35 | `trail.py:130` `if not isinstance(content, list): continue` (user msg early skip) | Defensive type guard. | Replicate. |
| B36 | `trail.py:156-161` `if isinstance(content, str): text = content` user-message branch | Robust against either content shape. | Replicate. |
| B37 | `trail.py:178-180` assistant `for blk in content: if blk.get("type") != "tool_use": continue` | Skips assistant text blocks entirely — assistant text is NEVER captured into the packet. | **AMBIGUOUS / load-bearing-by-omission.** Spec §4 says transcript_summary "from tool-use names + first lines of assistant messages" but py-reference does neither. Promoting this to Category A would also be valid; it sits here because the omission is consistent with py-reference's general strategy of "don't capture assistant prose". |

---

## Category C — Implementation gaps (spec specifies X; py-reference doesn't do it)

| # | Spec section | What spec requires | py-reference status | Recommendation |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| C1 | §3 `trail packet generate --latest` | Find the latest session for the cwd. | Not implemented. | TS adds (spec wins). |
| C2 | §3 `trail packet list` | List packets under `.trail/sessions/`. | Not implemented. | TS adds. |
| C3 | §3 `trail --version` | Print version. | Not implemented (argparse `--version` not declared). | TS adds. |
| C4 | §3 `trail --help` and per-subcommand `--help` | Top-level help on no args. | argparse exits 2 with usage on no args. | TS port to use commander/cac; provide proper help. |
| C5 | §3 `--llm-budget-usd <number>` flag | Plumbs the budget through to subprocess. | Hardcoded `0.50` at trail.py:369. | TS adds (spec wins; minor). |
| C6 | §3 `--output <path>` flag | Output directory for packet.yml + packet.md (default `.trail/sessions/<id>/`). | py-reference uses `--out` (singular file path or `-` for stdout); no directory mode; defaults to stdout. | TS adopts spec's `--output` (directory) + writes both files there. |
| C7 | §3 `--format yaml|md|both` flag | Selects rendering. | Not implemented (separate render.py CLI). | TS adds; default `both`. |
| C8 | §3 `--dry-run` flag | Compute + validate, no writes. | Not implemented. | TS adds; spec value is high (CI dogfooding). |
| C9 | §3 `--no-storage` flag | Skip Layer 2 row write. | Not applicable (no storage in py-reference). | TS adds when storage stub lands. |
| C10 | §3 `--quiet` flag | Suppress informational stderr. | Not implemented; verbose by default. | TS adds. |
| C11 | §3 `--strict-llm` flag | Exit 7 instead of falling back. | Not implemented; always falls back. | TS adds. |
| C12 | §4 default behavior step 3 "reads git state via simple-git" | Populates `pr.repository, pr.branch, pr.base_branch, pr.author`, `diff_summary.base_sha, head_sha, lines_added, lines_deleted`. | NOT IMPLEMENTED — all fields empty/zero in fixture. | **Highest-impact gap** (with A23). TS adds simple-git integration; parity fixture must be re-rolled OR these fields must remain empty in TS for parity OR spec relaxes to "best-effort, empty if not in a git repo". |
| C13 | §4 `_meta.schema_url` "constant pointer to URL" | A real URL. | py-reference uses relative path. | See A2. |
| C14 | §4 `_meta.packet_id` "ULID generated at packet-build time" | ULID format. | py-reference uses `uuid.uuid4()`. | TS uses ULID per spec; allowed parity diff. |
| C15 | §4 `_meta.generator.version` "from `package.json` version" | Semver from package.json. | py-reference uses literal `"0.1.0-dev"`. | TS reads package.json. |
| C16 | §4 `pr.author` from `git config user.email` | Git config read. | py-reference: empty. | TS adds. |
| C17 | §4 `agent_session.transcript_summary` (LLM or mechanical bullets) | Non-empty list. | py-reference always `[]`. | A3: pick. Recommend implement in TS (mechanical: list of unique tool_use names + first sentence of first assistant message). |
| C18 | §4 `diff_summary.semantic_changes[].excerpts` "bounded slices of before/after text from `~/.claude/file-history/`" | Reads from `~/.claude/file-history/<session-id>/*`. | py-reference reads `old_string`/`new_string`/`content` directly from tool_use input — does NOT consult `~/.claude/file-history/`. | **Spec wrong / over-specified.** py-reference's source (tool input fields) is sufficient for Edit/Write/MultiEdit; file-history is needed only if we want to verify what file-content snapshot was taken (i.e., what the file looked like BEFORE the agent modified it, even when no Edit was used). A3: relax spec to "from tool_use input"; file-history is a v0.2 enhancement. |
| C19 | §4 `commands_run[*].exit_code` from real exit code | Real value. | Always `0`. | See A13. |
| C20 | §4 `commands_run[*].duration_ms` | Real value. | Always `0`. | TS could read from tool_result if available (Claude Code surfaces durations on some tools). For parity, accept 0; for product value, attempt to parse. Recommend latter. |
| C21 | §4 `test_evidence.passed`, `failed`, `not_run` "AND their exit codes" | Tests classified by exit code. | Without exit codes, py-reference puts ALL tests in `passed`. | See A13. |
| C22 | §4 `provenance.authorship` "hook-tracked if Claude Code session hooks fired; heuristic otherwise" | Two-mode logic. | py-reference: constants. | See A26. |
| C23 | §4 `provenance.human_touched_files` "files modified during the session window but NOT touched by any tool use" | Heuristic via file mtimes. | py-reference: always `[]`. | A3: defer to v0.2 (matches py-reference); document. |
| C24 | §5 Layer 2 writes "first-12-chars-of-match" | 12-char snippet in validation_errors. | py-reference: 40 chars. | See A37. |
| C25 | §5 Layer 1 redacts `agent_session.transcript_summary[*]` | Required field for redaction. | py-reference field is always `[]`; redaction trivially applies. | OK by vacuum; if C17 is implemented, ensure redaction pipeline covers it. |
| C26 | §5 Layer 2 "Continue write (do not crash) — but flag the packet as suspect via stderr warning" | Stderr warning emitted. | py-reference: stderr summary line includes `validation_errors=N` count, but no special "suspect packet" warning when N>0. | TS adds explicit warning per spec. |
| C27 | §6 mechanical "one claim per `semantic_changes[*]` entry" + confidence gradient | Per-DIFF claims with `supported`/`partial`/`ungrounded` per-claim. | py-reference groups by file; all claims `supported`. | See A27, A30. |
| C28 | §6 LLM mode "5–15 claims" range | Quantity gate. | py-reference: one claim per file group (no fixed range). | See A32. |
| C29 | §6 LLM mode "validation on parse: every cited evidence ref MUST resolve" | Model emits IDs that must resolve. | py-reference doesn't trust the model with IDs — generates them post-hoc. | See A32. py-reference's design is sounder; spec §6 should adopt it. |
| C30 | §6 `summary.ungrounded_claim_count` "populated even when 0" | Always present. | py-reference at `trail.py:521`: present, value 0 in fixture. | OK — already implemented. |
| C31 | §7 schema validation "Pre-write structural pass" + "Post-build cross-reference pass" + "Fail → exit 5" | Two formal validation passes; fail-closed. | py-reference performs NEITHER pass. No `packet_version` check, no started_at≤ended_at check, no ID-uniqueness check, no evidence-ref-resolution check before write. Layer 2 redaction is the ONLY pre-write check. | **Major gap.** TS adds proper validation; spec wins. The fixture happens to be valid because the construction code never produces invalid packets, but there's no defensive check. |
| C32 | §7 "formal JSON Schema" deliverable at `schema/pr-change-packet.v0.1.schema.json` | File exists and is used by TS via Ajv. | File does not exist in repo; py-reference doesn't use one. | Phase 1 deliverable; TS port creates it. |
| C33 | §8 Specific exit codes 2–8 with friendly stderr messages | Exit codes 0, 2-8. | py-reference: only 0 and uncaught-exception-1 effectively. | See A61. |
| C34 | §9 Performance bound "< 500MB peak RSS" + "< 30s wall mechanical" | Performance contract. | py-reference: no benchmarks; reads entire transcript into memory. | Verify on M-series Mac during build; if breaches, TS gets streaming. |
| C35 | §4 default behavior step 7 "Writes packet.yml + packet.md to `.trail/sessions/<session-id>/`" | Single command writes both. | py-reference: `trail` writes packet.yml, separate `trail-render` writes packet.md. | TS unifies; spec wins. |
| C36 | §4 default behavior step 8 "Writes Layer 2 storage row" | Drizzle write to SQLite. | Not in py-reference. | Phase 1 deliverable per spec (or Phase 1.5 per spec's "seam must exist"). |
| C37 | §4 default behavior step 9 "Prints one-line summary to stderr" with `packet <packet-id> for session <session-id> ({N} claims, {M} redactions) → .trail/sessions/<session-id>/` | Specific format. | py-reference: different format (see A44). | A3 picks. |
| C38 | Appendix A module structure | Specific TS package layout. | N/A — Python flat-script layout. | Module structure is "proposed, not load-bearing". OK. |
| C39 | §13 Success criterion #5: Layer 2 catches deliberate-bypass injected secret | Test exists. | py-reference: no tests. | Phase 1 build adds. |
| C40 | §13 Success criterion #4: pattern_set_version equals YAML's version | Verified. | py-reference: `data.get("version", "0.0.0")` so the fallback path could leak a wrong value if YAML lacked a version. Currently YAML has `0.1.1`. | TS removes fallback per B3. |
| C41 | §10 parity test exists at `apps/capture/test/parity.test.ts` | Test file. | N/A. | Phase 1 build deliverable. |
| C42 | §3 invocation "from inside a git repo" | Cwd is a git repo. | py-reference doesn't check; runs anywhere. | TS adds repo-root detection (only relevant once C12 lands). Without git: exit 3 per spec §8. |

---

## Category D — Schema tensions surfaced (cross-cutting)

The blind spec §11 lists five "known schema tensions". Verifying each against py-reference:

| Spec §11 tension | py-reference behavior | Verdict |
|---|---|---|
| `synthesis_mode` per claim | py-reference adds **TWO** non-schema fields: `claims[].synthesis` (per claim) AND `_meta.claim_synthesis` (run-level). They are partially redundant. | **Confirmed tension.** A3 picks one place (recommend `claims[].synthesis_mode` per claim — drops to mechanical for the test-rollup claim, so per-claim is correct). |
| Risk classification field | py-reference: not implemented; not populated; no `claims[].risk` field exists in fixture. | **Confirmed deferred.** Stage AB target. Spec is right to defer. |
| Approval trail field | py-reference: not implemented. | **Confirmed deferred.** Stage AB target. |
| Excerpt redaction policy | py-reference REDACTS excerpts (`trail.py:227, 237, 243, 255, 261`). The spec deliberately says NOT to redact excerpts. | **Spec ↔ py-reference disagree.** A3 must pick. Per the rationale in spec §5 ("file-content slices are gated by user's commit decision; Layer 3 catches actual secrets"), spec's logic is sound — but the parity fixture has redacted excerpts. Recommend: TS adopts spec (don't redact excerpts) AND parity fixture is regenerated; OR TS matches py-reference and spec §5 is rewritten. The first is the better product; the second is the lower-friction path. **Decide explicitly.** |
| `--patterns <path>` flag | py-reference: not implemented; patterns path is hardcoded. | **Confirmed deferred.** OK. |

### Additional schema tensions surfaced in this audit (not in §11)

| # | Tension | Where it surfaces | Recommendation |
|---|---|---|---|
| D6 | `_meta.capture_method` and `_meta.claim_synthesis` are emitted by py-reference but NOT defined in schema. | trail.py:451, 563. | A3: extend schema additively (v0.1.1) to declare these as optional `_meta` fields. |
| D7 | `test_evidence.passed[].cmd_ref` field is emitted but NOT in schema. | trail.py:206. | A3: extend schema. |
| D8 | Schema says `test_evidence.passed/failed/not_run` exist; py-reference always classifies tests as passed (zero exit codes assumed). | trail.py:196-207. | A3: either (a) accept the limitation and document, (b) parse exit codes from tool_result text. |
| D9 | Schema requires `started_at ≤ ended_at`; py-reference uses string-lex compare on timestamps; doesn't actually enforce. | trail.py:146-151. | A3: TS port enforces in validation pass. |
| D10 | Schema says "agent_session.session_id … if exposed by hooks" (line 58). py-reference always uses the .jsonl filename; treats session_id as required. | trail.py:472. | OK; lock spec to "always present from filename" and update schema comment. |
| D11 | Schema requires "pattern_set_version non-empty"; py-reference defaults to `"0.0.0"` if YAML missing. The packet would still validate but the `0.0.0` is misleading. | trail.py:47. | A3: TS removes fallback; fail loudly. |
| D12 | Excerpt `kind` values include `before#1`, `before#2`, ..., `after#1`, ... for MultiEdit. Schema example only documents `before` / `after`. | trail.py:254-262. | A3: extend schema example to include the `before#N`/`after#N` form (or normalize to a single `before`/`after` and emit a `hunk_index` field instead). |
| D13 | Excerpt elision string differs across spec / schema / py-reference. | A10. | A3: pick one canonical string. |
| D14 | `diff_summary.semantic_changes[].operation` lowercase enum. Schema example shows `operation: "edit"`; py-reference emits lowercased tool name including `"multiedit"`. Schema doesn't explicitly enumerate values. | trail.py:498. | A3: lock the enum to `{"write","edit","multiedit"}` and add to schema validation. |
| D15 | Schema rule "At least one of (semantic_changes, commands_run, test_evidence) MUST be non-empty" — not enforced anywhere by py-reference. | (no validation pass). | TS port enforces. |

---

## Final summary — high-priority items for Stage A3

Ranked by impact on parity oracle / product correctness:

1. **A23/C12 — Git state population.** Spec mandates simple-git for `pr.*` and `diff_summary.*`; py-reference populates none. Decide the parity-fixture story first (re-roll vs. accept divergence); the answer drives the whole TS-port test plan. This is the single biggest gap.
2. **A27/C27 — Mechanical claim grouping & confidence.** Spec says one claim per DIFF with confidence gradient; py-reference groups by file with constant `supported`. The fixture's 27 claims would balloon to ~104 under the spec's rule. Pick one before TS code is written.
3. **D-row "excerpt redaction policy" / spec §11.** Spec says no, py-reference says yes. The parity fixture has redacted excerpts. If TS implements spec, fixture regenerates; if TS implements py-reference, spec rewrites. Decide explicitly.
4. **A32/C29 — LLM synthesis design.** py-reference's design (LLM picks text; code picks evidence by file→DIFF mapping) is sounder than spec's design (LLM picks both, with post-validation). Spec should adopt py-reference's pattern; preserves spec's "model never invents IDs" invariant by construction.
5. **A38 + A10 + A11 — YAML serialization byte-identity.** For `--no-llm` parity, the TS port must emit byte-identical YAML (sort order, line width 120, default_flow_style=false, allow_unicode=true) AND match the elision-marker string EXACTLY (head=MAX-200, tail=150, marker `\n... [elided N chars] ...\n`). Single biggest source of latent parity breakage.
6. **A12 — Test detection regex.** Specific list including project-private `validate_canvas.py` / `validate-template.sh`. Either lock parity-exact, or pick a portable list and re-roll fixture.
7. **A13/C19/C21/D8 — Exit-code classification of tests.** py-reference treats every test as passed (exit_code=0). Spec says exit-code-driven. If TS implements spec, fixture's `test_evidence.passed[]` may move entries to `failed[]`/`not_run[]`. Either upgrade and re-roll, or relax spec.
8. **C31/C33/A61 — Validation passes & exit codes.** py-reference has neither a structural validation pass nor a cross-reference pass nor proper exit-code mapping. The spec's exit codes 2–8 are largely aspirational. TS port must add real validation; spec's exit-code text is correct, no rewrite needed.
9. **A4/D6/D7/A31/D14 — Schema additive bump (v0.1.1).** `_meta.capture_method`, `_meta.claim_synthesis`, `claims[].synthesis_mode`, `test_evidence.passed[].cmd_ref`, `operation` enum: all currently emitted-but-not-declared. A schema bump cleans this up without breaking the v0.1 contract for protocol adopters.
10. **A18/A52/A19 — modules_touched / files_changed / single-file-per-DIFF rules.** All currently de-facto in py-reference but undocumented. Lock to spec text; TS replicates exactly.
11. **A6/A8/A21/A48/A73 — Prompt normalization pipeline (tag-strip → check-empty → 1200-truncate → redact, plus `\s+→ ' '` collapse for task_intent.summary).** Hidden in py-reference; required for prompt parity. Document in spec §4 and §5.
12. **A45/C1 — `--latest` and cwd-scoped session search.** Required for the spec's default behavior; py-reference is cwd-insensitive. Add to TS; small but user-facing.
13. **C18 — file-history source.** Spec over-specifies; relax to "from tool_use input fields" for v0.1; document `~/.claude/file-history/` as a v0.2 enhancement (verifying snapshot consistency).
14. **A50 — Model field semantics.** py-reference: last assistant model wins. Spec: first wins. Pick. Recommend last (matches fixture).
15. **A26/C22/C23 — provenance authorship constants.** py-reference uses constants; spec implies heuristics. Either keep constants (lock) or implement heuristics (more work, marginal value for v0.1).

If 1, 2, 3, and 5 alone go wrong, the parity oracle test will not pass on a single run. Items 6, 7, 8 will break it on edge-case fixtures. Items 4, 9, 10, 11 produce drift the parity oracle won't catch but reviewers will notice.

---

**End of A2 audit.** Next: A3 reconciles. The recommendations above are opinionated suggestions; the human reviewer chooses.
